label: Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models

node.label: Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models
node.definition: Normalizing flows use invertible transformations with tractable density; rectified and flow-matching models instead learn continuous transport vector fields.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models is modeled as depending on Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields, Flow matching & transport objectives and feeding downstream nodes such as Image generation, editing & design, Video generation & simulation synthesis, Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting.
examples: atlas downstream link to Image generation, editing & design
atlas downstream link to Video generation & simulation synthesis
atlas downstream link to Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields, Flow matching & transport objectives.

dependencies.rationale: Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Continuous normalizing flows & learned vector fields.

In this atlas graph, Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Flow matching & transport objectives because Flow matching & transport objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
In this atlas graph, Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models has a `composes` dependency on Differentiable ODE/PDE/SDE solvers with `common` strength.
